report: Phase 29.1 — Pre-Send Contact Verification + Score Upgrade Report
executive_summary: 4 candidates verified via browser + HTTP. 1 candidate replaced (UW Extension → MoneyFit.org). Strict >=90 scoring applied. 2 candidates qualified (Budgets Are Sexy: 93, MoneyFit.org: 90). 2 held (GreenPath: 83, Credit.org: 81). 0 sent — awaiting user approval.

excellent: Budgets Are Sexy — contact form + J. Money responds to all
MoneyFit.org — contactus@ + named press contacts (Rick Munster, Todd Christensen)
medium: GreenPath — mediarelations@greenpath.com (press email, not tool offers)
replaced: UW Extension — target page redirected, generic info@ contact

key_evidence_findings: Budgets Are Sexy: Page teaches Assets-Liabilities net worth method, offers spreadsheet + Personal Capital + Mint.com, NO free interactive visual projector. Gap confirmed.

MoneyFit.org: Has 'Debt Repayment Calculators' section with Credit Card Debt and Debt Management calculators. NO debt snowball calculator anywhere on page ('snowball' search = FALSE). 19 calculator headings, 50 calc-related links — comprehensive but missing snowball.
GreenPath: '(NOTICE: We are currently working on updating the accessibility of these tools)' — gold-standard evidence of active tool upgrades
Credit.org: 30+ calculators verified, debt snowball absent — good evidence but contact quality caps score

current_status.positives: 2 real prospects qualified at >=90 with verified contacts and evidence
current_status.negatives: 78 emails sent, 0 replies, 0 backlinks, 0 embeds, 0 content clicks

next_actions: Await user approval to send 2 qualified messages
Jun 19: V2 test batch reply deadline
After 7 days from send: evaluate V3 microtest reply rate
success_condition_status: PARTIAL — Phase 29.1 produced 2 real evidence-backed prospects at >=90. Sending them would complete Phase 29.1. If these 2 produce >=1 reply, Phase 29 succeeds. If 0 replies, the channel remains unvalidated and further pivot is needed.
lessons_learned: Contact path quality is the #1 score blocker. GreenPath has the best evidence (accessibility notice) but worst contact (mediarelations@). Evidence can't compensate for weak contact.
Named individuals (Rick Munster, Todd Christensen, J. Money) dramatically improve reply probability scores vs generic inboxes.
Specific page content extraction (exact calculator names, exact section names) is critical for evidence quality scoring. Generic 'has calculators' is not enough.
VPS search engine blocks prevent finding new prospects via web search. Direct URL testing and browser navigation are the only reliable methods from this IP.
